title: pleroma mello-barretoi
intro_study: According to "Photodermatology, photoimmunology & photomedicine", Pleroma mello-barretoi (Polypodium leucotomos) has been found to have photoprotective benefits, including increasing the UV dose required for immediate pigment darkening (IPD), minimal erythema dose (MED), and minimal phototoxic dose (MPD), making it a potentially useful agent for systemic photoprotection and adjunct treatment in photochemotherapy and phototherapy.
side_effects: Pleroma mello-barretoi can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its potent neurotoxic properties. Prolonged exposure to the plant's compounds can also cause muscle weakness, tremors, and seizures in some individuals. In rare cases, improper use of the plant has been linked to respiratory failure and even death, making it a highly toxic substance that requires careful handling. If ingested or inhaled in excessive amounts, pleroma mello-barretoi can lead to long-term damage to the nervous system and other vital organs.
